calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Eve Ferguson'S Motion To Quash Subpoena
Off Calendar Jun-21-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ruling: Plaintiff Eve Ferguson's Motion To Quash Subpoena is taken off calendar. Motion to be reset in San Bernardino County. Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Continued To Aug-23-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar May-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER A. JAVANMARDI OF THE JAVANMARDI LAW, P.C., TEL. NO. 1-818-714-1634 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KEVIN D. REESE OF THE OGLETREE DEAKINS INC, TEL. NO. 1-415-536-3407 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Dignity Health's motion to change venue to San Bernardino County based on convenience of the witnesses is granted. While in the usual case the convenience of party witnesses are not considered, that does not apply when the party witnesses are likely to be called by the adverse party. (Hardon v. Kinner & Hammond (1955) 130 Cal.App.2d 750, 757.) The convenience of former employees of a party may also be taken into account. (Edward v. Pierson (1957) 156 Cal.App.2d 72, 76.) Dignity Health has shown good cause to grant the motion. Ms. Findlay and Ms. Gatson, former employees of Dignity Health, are key witnesses regarding the decision to terminate plaintiff Eve Ferguson and they clearly state that travelling to San Francisco would be an inconvenience. (Findlay Dec., pars. 4 and 6; Gatson Dec., pars. 3 and 5-7.) Ms. Ferguson seeks to take the depositions of many of the employee witnesses and those witnesses state that a San Francisco trial would be a burden. Ms. Ferguson makes only a minimal showing opposing this motion. Many of the witnesses that she identifies appear to be peripheral to the key issues in this case. Moreover, many of them reside in Arizona and Ms. Ferguson fails to show how it is more convenient for them to travel to San Francisco than to travel to San Bernardino County. All transfer fees must be paid by Dignity Health. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
